headline: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
description: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

'For sale, 5-year-old child, docile and calm': Yazidi genocide trial dives into horrors of IS
description: The Paris criminal court heard the harrowing reality of the enslavement of a people in the name of religion. Rarely has a terrorism trial gone so far in exposing the atrocities perpetrated by jihadists.

headline: Paris mayoral election: Three candidates head to second round following Knafo's withdrawal
description: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire has refused any alliance with radical-left candidate Sophia Chikirou, as conservative candidate Rachida Dati looks to benefit from the withdrawal of two candidates.

headline: Rosalía launches her world tour in Lyon
description: The Spanish pop star kicked off her Lux World Tour on Monday at the LDLC Arena, on the outskirts of Lyon, in a whirlwind of style, language and religious imagery.

headline: Mark Carney's delicate balancing act at Canada's helm
description: The pragmatic former banker captivated the world with his speech in Davos on the autonomy of middle powers in the face of hegemonic giants. But the political footing of this tightrope walker remains difficult to define.

Five takeaways from the first round of French local elections
description: The 2026 municipal elections have already concluded in the vast majority of French towns, with a mayor being directly elected in the first round. In larger cities, however, the right and the Socialists are in the lead for the second round.

headline: French local elections: First-round results give sense of political landscape one year before presidential vote
description: With blurred lines between the right and far right, rivalry between the radical left and the Socialists and the fading of the presidential party, the period between the two rounds of the municipal vote is a testing ground for political power balances ahead of the 2027 election.

headline: In Paris suburbs, LFI won the biggest prize, but made few gains
description: La France Insoumise won Saint-Denis, the largest Paris suburb, in the first round. Yet, the result masks disappointing results in other working-class areas that the radical left has specifically targeted.
